meaning: n.(在月球东北象限的)开普勒环形山(直径为35公里)
examples: During the Renaissance, the great minds of Nicolaus Copernicus, Johannes Kepler and Galileo Galilei demonstrated the power of scientific study and discovery.
But that their minds are not different from our own is demonstrated by the fact that the hard-won discoveries of scientists like Kepler or Einstein become the commonplace knowledge of schoolchildren.
Tony Kepler raises killer snakes for a living and he loves his job.
"It was all fraud, " said Dirk Becker, a banking analyst at Frankfurt-based Kepler Equities.
They orbit the Kepler-62 star in the Constellation Lyra - 1, 200 light-years from Earth.
